chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C.P.No.D-1088/2025, C.P.No.D-814/2017, C.P.No.D-31/2020, C.P.No.D-609/2022, C.P.No.D-893/2022, C.P.No.D-979/2022, C.P.No.D-193/2023,C.P.No.D-24/2024, C.P.No.D-361/2024, C.P.No.D-363/2024, C.P.No.D-453/2024, C.P.No.D-1002/2025, C.P.No.D-1035/2025, C.P.No.D-1037/2025, C.P.No.D-1042/2025, C.P.No.D-1047/2025, C.P.No.D-1057/2025, C.P.No.D-1150/2025, C.P.No.D-1166/2025, C.P.No.D-1180/2025, C.P.No.D-1181/2025, C.P.No.D-1186/2025, C.P.No.D-1187/2025, C.P.No.D-1205/2025, C.P.No.D-1210/2025, C.P.No.D-1213/2025, C.P.No.D-1227/2025, C.P.No.D-1239/2025, C.P.No.D-1256/2025, C.P.No.D-1295/2025, C.P.No.D-408/2025, C.P.No.D-432/2025, C.P.No.D-435/2025, C.P.No.D-501/2025, C.P.No.D-552/2025, C.P.No.D-568/2025, C.P.No.D-659/2025, C.P.No.D-681/2025, C.P.No.D-714/2025, C.P.No.D-715/2025, C.P.No.D-807/2025, C.P.No.D-863/2025, C.P.No.D-873/2025, C.P.No.D-994/2025, C.P.No.D-10/2026,C.P.No.D-02/2026, C.P.No.D-52/2026, C.P.No.D-54/2026, C.P.No.D-65/2026. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 01. For orders on office objection "A" 02. For hearing of main case. 10.02.2026 M/S. Habibullah Ghouri, Ashfaque Hussain Abro, Faiz Muhammad Larik, Tufail Ahmed Kumbar, Imtiaz Ali Mugheri, Abdul Sami Soomro, Abdul Rehman Bhutto, Muneeb Ahmed Dhamraho, Manzoor Ali Kalhoro, Mansoor Ali Panhwar, Zuhaib Azam Rajput, Rashid Ali Tunio, Nisar Ahmed Abro, Rano Soomro, Ayaz Hussain Kalhoro, Inam Rehman Abro, Atta Hussain Qadri, Javed Ahmed Soomro, Muhammad Ismail Jatoi, Shakeel Ahmed Ansari, Bahadur Ali Shahani, Aijaz Ali Kalhoro, Riaz Hussain Khoso, Abdul Qudoos Jatoi, Noor Ahmed Lashari, Kaleemullah Khoso, Wajid Ali Gaad, Zubair Ahmed Kalhoro, Mujahid Ali Jatoi, Muhammad Afzal Jagirani, Syed Soofan Shah, Ali Raza Pathan, Advocates for the petitioners.

For hearing of main case. 10.02.2026 M/S. Habibullah Ghouri, Ashfaque Hussain Abro, Faiz Muhammad Larik, Tufail Ahmed Kumbar, Imtiaz Ali Mugheri, Abdul Sami Soomro, Abdul Rehman Bhutto, Muneeb Ahmed Dhamraho, Manzoor Ali Kalhoro, Mansoor Ali Panhwar, Zuhaib Azam Rajput, Rashid Ali Tunio, Nisar Ahmed Abro, Rano Soomro, Ayaz Hussain Kalhoro, Inam Rehman Abro, Atta Hussain Qadri, Javed Ahmed Soomro, Muhammad Ismail Jatoi, Shakeel Ahmed Ansari, Bahadur Ali Shahani, Aijaz Ali Kalhoro, Riaz Hussain Khoso, Abdul Qudoos Jatoi, Noor Ahmed Lashari, Kaleemullah Khoso, Wajid Ali Gaad, Zubair Ahmed Kalhoro, Mujahid Ali Jatoi, Muhammad Afzal Jagirani, Syed Soofan Shah, Ali Raza Pathan, Advocates for the petitioners. Mr. Liaquat Ali Shar, Additional Advocate General, Sindh alongwith Mohsan Raza Dasti, ADC-II, on behalf of Deputy Commissioner, Larkana, Abdul Jabbar Butt, A.E.O, Focal Person (DEO) Primary Larkana, Allah Bux Soomro, D.E.O (Elementary/Secondary/H.S), Kamber Shahdadkot, Siraj Ahmed O.S/Focal Person on behalf of Deputy Commissioner Larkana, Naeem Ahmed Ansari, XEN, S.E Work and Services Larkana, Dr. Rakesh Lal on behalf of D.G Health, Hyderabad, Dr.Shoukat Ali Abro, D.H.O Larkana, Muhammad Ishaq Bhurgri, District Population Officer, Larkana, Saeed Ahmed Jokhio, Mukhtiarkar Sijawal on behalf of D.C Kamber, Muneer Ahmed Bhutto, A.E.N Buildings Kamber on behalf of Executive Engineer and Abdul Ghani Pitafi, Mukhtiarkar Kandhkot on behalf of Deputy Commissioner, Kashmore @ Kandhkot. *.*.*.*.*.*.*.*.*.*.*.*.*.* The order dated 22.01.2026, passed in C.P.No.D-52/2026, C.P.No.D-54/2026 and C.P.No.D-432/2025, which are similar petitions, is as follows;- All these Petitioners seek employment in Government service on the quota reserved for disabled persons under the Sindh Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2018 and Sindh Civil Servants Act, 1973. The Sindh Act of 2018, which is the special law on the subject, provides inter alia: “11. Authority shall establish a „Special Employment Exchange and portal‟, to which every establishment shall furnish such information as maybe prescribed by the Government in relation to vacancies earmarked for “Persons with Disabilities” which have occurred and filled or are about to occur. 11. The Grievance Redressal Officer shall maintain a register of complaints in the manner as maybe prescribed by the Authority, and every complaint shall be inquired within two weeks of its registration.

Authority shall establish a „Special Employment Exchange and portal‟, to which every establishment shall furnish such information as maybe prescribed by the Government in relation to vacancies earmarked for “Persons with Disabilities” which have occurred and filled or are about to occur. 11. The Grievance Redressal Officer shall maintain a register of complaints in the manner as maybe prescribed by the Authority, and every complaint shall be inquired within two weeks of its registration. If the aggrieved persons is not satisfied with the action taken on his/her complaint, he or she may approach the Authority. 11. The Government shall reserve a quota of five percent (5%) at various levels for the „Persons with Disabilities‟ in the Government departments, institutions, entities, and corporate entities owned and managed by the Government. The Government shall through notification provide for such relaxation upper age limit for employment of persons with disabilities as it thinks fit.” Furthermore, Section 28 of the Sindh Act of 2018 established an Authority, namely the Sindh Persons with Disabilities Protection Authority (SPDPA), for implementing and enforcing the provisions of said Act. It appears to us that if aforesaid provisions are implemented in letter and spirit, grievance of most of such Petitioners can be redressed at the departmental level. Therefore, Learned AAG Sindh shall take instructions and inform the Court whether the SPDPA is functional, and if so, whether the Special Employment Exchange and Portal envisaged in Section 11 of the Act has been deployed to facilitate employment of disabled persons. The Sindh Persons with Disabilities Protection Authority (SPDPA) is a necessary party to these petitions. It is therefore added as a Respondent. Learned counsel for Petitioners shall file an amended title accordingly. Thereafter, notice be issued to the SPDPA through its Director General or Regional Director, At Larkana. The aforesaid order is passed in these petitions. The petitioners shall file amended title to array Sindh Persons with Disabilities Protection Authority (SPDPA) as the respondents in these petitions, where-after the notices be issued to said authority.

The aforesaid order is passed in these petitions. The petitioners shall file amended title to array Sindh Persons with Disabilities Protection Authority (SPDPA) as the respondents in these petitions, where-after the notices be issued to said authority. Pursuant to order dated 22.01.2026, Mohsin Raza, Additional Deputy Commissioner-II, Larkana, on behalf of Deputy Commissioner, Larkana, states to the best of his knowledge that the Authority (SPDPA) envisaged in Section 28 of the Sindh Act, 2018, has not been notified. Learned Additional Advocate General, Sindh, seeks time to seek instructions in that regard from the Chief Secretary, Sindh, and to file the comments in that regard. The comments on behalf on behalf of Chief Secretary, Sindh, shall also apprise the Court of steps taken to implement the Provisions of Section 11 of the Sindh Act, 2018, particularly Sub Section 9 and 10 thereof (reproduced in order dated 22.01.2026). Mr. Abdul Rehman Bhutto, Advocate, files Vakalatnama on behalf of petitioner in C.P.No.D-24/2024 while Mr. Sarfraz Ali Abbasi, Advocate, files Vakalatnama on behalf of respondents No.1 to 3 in C.P.No.D-814/2017; the same are taken on record. To come up on 25.02.2026. Copy of this order shall be provided to learned Additional Advocate General, Sindh and also be e-mailed to the Chief Secretary, Government of Sindh. JUDGE JUDGE
